meaning: v.驱散,(使)(云、雾、疑虑等)消散,浪费(金钱或时间)
examples: Was she the pure phantom I loved because I would never touch her, never allow my all too real hands to dissipate the mist?
As the uncertainty and fear of investors dissipate, opportunities will bubble to the surface.
The excited water molecules dissipate their energy by bashing against the enamel and bone.
